Defines maximum supported MTU sizes for Bluetooth communication based on the device controller.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines the maximum supported MTU (Maximum Transmission Unit) size for Bluetooth communication, conditional on the type of Bluetooth controller being used. It uses a preprocessor directive `#pragma once` to ensure the file is included only once in a single compilation. The file checks if the `BT_CONTROLLER_DA14681` macro is defined. If it is, the `ATT_MAX_SUPPORTED_MTU` is set to 339 bytes, which is optimized for Android devices that support larger MTUs. Otherwise, the MTU is set to 158 bytes, which is compatible with iOS 8, as this is the maximum payload size allowed by iOS 8 for legacy products. This configuration allows the system to optimize Bluetooth throughput based on the device's capabilities.
